old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-vsph","title":"[cross-impl] Trickle reset guard uses divergent proxies across Rust/Python/C","description":"Reviewed last 20 commits (bd auto-updates + a50ae4188 trickle.c alignment). Rust uses state==Stopped || interval!=imin; Python uses _generation==0 || interval!=imin; C uses transmit_time==0 || interval!=imin (post a50ae4188). Same RFC 6206 reset algorithm but different sentinel values risk divergent behavior on time=0, overflow, or init edge cases (see project-LICHEN-67ca). Test vectors in test/vectors/ and rpl tests should be extended for cross-impl determinism. Matches focus on \"same algorithm different behavior\".","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"agent@lichen-project.dev","created_at":"2026-07-23T02:44:44.007322901Z","created_by":"AI Agent","updated_at":"2026-07-23T02:44:44.007322901Z"}
